title: Slyssk

{{Quote|Slyssk may not be the best starship thief in the galaxy - but he's certainly the fastest!|Marn Hierogryph|<ref name="KOTOR31:T">[[Knights of the Old Republic 31|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 31]]</ref>}}
'''Slyssk''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] ship thief, who joined [[Marn Hierogryph]] and [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Zayne Carrick]] through their adventures in the [[Mandalorian Wars]].

==Biography==
===Stealing a ship to Serroco===
{{Quote|I know I'm not the toughest Trandoshan around. Hunts make you nauseous—you go into piracy. That's life.|Slyssk|<ref name="KOTOR13:DoF1" />}}
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]], Slyssk lived around the [[Old Republic era|time]] of the [[Mandalorian Wars]],<ref name="KOTOR13:DoF1" /> a major conflict between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the nomadic [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warriors.<ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> A core part of Trandoshan culture was [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] but partaking in it left Slyysk feeling nauseous so he choose an alternate career as a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] thief. Slyssk joined the [[Raff Syndicate]] where he learned how to steal ships,<ref name="KOTORH" /> but he soon became disenfranchised with the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] group<ref name="KOTOR37:PM2">[[Knights of the Old Republic 37|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 37]]</ref> and ended up being kicked out of their ranks before he could actually learn how to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] the craft he stole.<ref name="KOTORH" />

[[File:Slyssk.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Slyssk on Serroco]]

Slyssk's skills were later enlisted when [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] [[black marketeer]], [[Marn Hierogryph]], and [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Zayne Carrick]]—both of whom were on the run from the [[Jedi Covenant]] after being falsely accused as the perpetrators of the [[Padawan Massacre]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]—were looking for passage off the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ralltiir/Legends|Ralltiir]]. Hierogryph arranged for Slyssk to steal a ship that would be delivered to [[Pad 223]] at [[Time/Legends|midnight]] for the sum of 9000 credits. The Trandoshan's target ship was the ''[[Little Bivoli]]'', a [[Quartermaster-class supply carrier|''Quartermaster''-class supply carrier]] owned by a [[Little Bivoli's original crew|crew]] of [[Fringer/Legends|freelance provisioners]] who assisted the overwhelmed supply channels of the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]].<ref name="KOTOR13:DoF1" /> While the owners were preparing to [[Fuel/Legends|refuel]] the ship, Slyssk stole the ''Bivoli''<ref name="KOTOR15:DoF3">[[Knights of the Old Republic 15|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 15]]</ref> and flew it to Pad 223, damaging several [[Lamp/Legends|lampposts]] in the process of landing the craft. He hid inside the ship upon seeing Hierogryph and Carrick waiting, but the timid Trandoshan eventually emerged only to demand ten times his original price. He reasoned that Hierogryph's notoriety warranted the increase, but negotiations between the two [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] were proving futile so Carrick used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to bring one of the damaged lampposts down onto Slyssk. Hierogryph pushed the Trandoshan out of harm's way in time—an act which Slyssk saw fit to swear a [[Life debt/Legends|life debt]] for, naming the Snivvian as his [[ghrakhowsk]]—to let them have the ship for free under the terms of Slyssk's life debt. The original crew then arrived and attempted to recover their ship, forcing the trio to get aboard the ship and take off. Upon arriving in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], the ''Bivoli'' was hailed by the [[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|''Courageous'']], [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of a Republic [[Battle Group Serroco|fleet]], and ordered to join formation for a [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump to [[Serroco]] where a [[Battle of Serroco|confrontation]] with the Mandalorians was forthcoming.<ref name="KOTOR13:DoF1" />

The ''Bivoli'' landed on Serroco at [[Camp Three (Serroco)|Camp Three]], a staging ground for the [[Republic Army]], where Hierogryph opened the ship as an alternative to the army commissary. Slyssk was employed as the [[Chef/Legends|chef]], with his [[Bivoli Tempari]] gaining a positive reputation among the [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] and highlighting the ''Bivoli'' as "the place to be in Camp Three". Carrick, meanwhile, insisted that Slyssk and Hierogryph leave the planet on the basis of a [[Force vision/Legends|premonition]] he had of the impending Mandalorian attack and left for the ''Courageous'' to try and warn the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]].<ref name="KOTOR14:DoF2">[[Knights of the Old Republic 14|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 14]]</ref> On the next day—the same day Carrick had predicted the battle would take place—Slyssk prepared [[Flatcake/Legends|Trandoshani flatcakes]] using an improved method of his that made the food particularly good. Despite Carrick pleading with Hierogryph via [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] for them to leave, the Snivvian kept the ''Bivoli'' open into the afternoon and only made ready to leave after a warning siren sounded the arrival of Mandalorian vessels in the [[Star system/Legends|system]]. At this point, Slyssk informed Hierogryph that, because he had stolen the ship before it was completely refueled, there was not enough fuel to leave the planet.<ref name="KOTOR15:DoF3" /> The Trandoshan soon suffered a panic attack, seized his ''ghrakhowsk'', and ran aboard a delayed troop [[Transport/Legends|transport]]. Forcing himself past the transport's guards, Slyssk took the helm and launched the ship to safety. It later transpired that the transport's flight crew had been asleep, and thus Slyssk had inadvertently saved half a [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] of [[Republic soldier]]s. When met on [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]] by members of the [[Ministry of Defense]], a propaganda story was built around Hierogryph and Slyssk using the identities of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Benegryph Goodvalor]] and [[Slyssk|Commander True]], Goodvalor's "trusty Trandoshan sidekick",<ref name="KOTOR47:Demon1" /> respectively.<ref name="47notes">{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor47.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #47''}}</ref>

===Serving his ''ghrakhowsk''===
{{Quote|Master Gryph sent me to buy a ship on Coruscant—only I had never ''bought'' a ship—only…''borrowed'' them. I got nervous.|Slyssk|<ref name="KOTOR37:PM2" />}}
Eventually, the pair were picked up by the [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]], [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunting]] [[Moomo Brothers]] by the order of [[Lhosan Industries]]' chairman, [[Jervo Thalien]], for a task on the planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]].<ref name="KOTOR40:DA2">[[Knights of the Old Republic 40|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 40]]</ref> While Hierogryph led the [[Taris Resistance]] against the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] who had [[Taris Siege|invaded]] the planet, Slyssk and [[Dob Moomo]] retrieved Carrick from the [[First Battle of Omonoth|beleaguered]] ''[[Arkanian Legacy]]'' and brought him, as well as [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Darth Malak|Alek]]; Mandalorian [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Demagol]], disguised as [[Rohlan Dyre]]; and [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|Arkanian Offshoot]] [[Jarael]], back to Taris.<ref name="KOTOR21:DoH3">[[Knights of the Old Republic 21|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 21]]</ref> Arriving on Taris, Carrick jumped from the Moomo's ship, ''[[Moomo Williwaw]]'', and met with Hierogryph, leaving Slyssk and the rest of the group<ref name="KOTOR22:KoS1">[[Knights of the Old Republic 22|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 22]]</ref> to find a place in orbit to wait.<ref name="KotOR23:KoS2">[[Knights of the Old Republic 23|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 23]]</ref>

Around the same time as the Mandalorian occupation of Taris, a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] placed upon Slyysk for charges of piracy totalling 40,000 credits was advertised in ''[[The Adjudicator Special Report: The Colonies]]'' trade publication.<ref name="TASR:Colonies">{{KOTORbackups|23}}</ref> The resistance on Taris ultimately failed<ref name="KOTOR24:KoS3">[[Knights of the Old Republic 24|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 24]]</ref> so Slyssk and the ''Williwaw'' escorted [[Sector Constable]] [[Noana Sowrs]]'s children safely to [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] before hastening to rescue Carrick and Hierogryph from the [[Rakghoul]]–[[Disaster on Jebble|infested]] planet of [[Jebble]].<ref name="KOTOR28:Vec4">[[Knights of the Old Republic 28|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 28]]</ref> Slyysk remained with the crew during their travels to the planet [[Odryn]] where part of the group retrieved [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]] from a Jedi Covenant [[Sanctum of the Exalted|storehouse]] with which they could expose the Covenant.<ref name="KOTOR30:E2">[[Knights of the Old Republic 30|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 30]]</ref>

[[File:Slyssk_a_Raff_captain.jpg|thumb|right|120px|Slyssk disguised as a Raff Syndicate captain]]

The ''Williwaw'' arrived at [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], home to the [[Draay Estate|base]] of the Jedi Covenant and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] as a whole, to find its path blocked by a [[Vanjervalis Chain|formation]] of Republic [[Warship/Legends|warships]]. Attempting to run the [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] led by [[Swiftsure (Inexpugnable-class)|''Swiftsure'']], the ship was heavily damaged, forcing the crew to try a different plan. Purposefully crashing the ''Williwaw'' into [[Swiftsure (Inexpugnable-class)|''Swiftsure'']]{{'s}} [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]], Slyysk stole aboard a [[Conductor-class short-haul landing craft|''Conductor''-class]] [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] and, using the discord caused by the ''Williwaw''{{'s}} crash as cover, ferried Carrick and Hierogryph to the planet below. Slyysk had a panic attack after landing which forced Hierogryph to give the Trandoshan some [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] and send him to a [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]] to rest.<ref name="KOTOR31:T" /> Hierogryph and Carrick were [[Vindication|successful]] in clearing their names with the Jedi, bringing an end to the Jedi Covenant in the process, and set out to stop those who made life poorer for others.<ref name="KOTOR35:Vin4">[[Knights of the Old Republic 35|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 35]]</ref> While Carrick took a leave of absence, Hierogryph sent Slyysk to buy a new starship to replace the ''Williwaw''. As he had only ever stolen ships previously, the Trandoshan became nervous at the prospect of legally buying one and so he bought what was described to him as "a gem of a ship". What he did not realize was that the ship, called ''[[Hot Prospect]]'', was in fact a [[Calipsan 560 mining ship|Calipsan 560]] gem mining ship infested with [[Mynock/Legends|mynocks]] and generally in poor condition. In return for being fooled into buying the sub-par ship, Hierogryph used his position as Slyysk's ''ghrakhowsk'' to make the Trandoshan work on cleaning up the ship.<ref name="KOTOR37:PM2" />

The crew of the ''Hot Prospect'', still minus Carrick, went to the [[Planetary Futures Exchanges|planetary rights auction]] being held at the [[Metellos Exchange]] with the intention of scamming it. Their ploy was uncovered by the [[Chevin/Legends|Chevin]] manager of the Raff Syndicate-backed Metellos Exchange, [[Nunk Plaarvin]],<ref name="KOTOR36:PM1">[[Knights of the Old Republic 36|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 36]]</ref> leading to the capture of Jarael and Demagol. Carrick returned and met up with Hierogryph and Slyysk aboard the ''Hot Prospect'' where they formulated a plan to rescue their friends by having the Trandoshan pretend to be a Raff [[Captain/Legends|captain]], complete with a suitable outfit that recycled the ''Prospect''{{'s}} mynocks as kneepads. The trio confronted Plaarvin and managed to intimidate him into leaving for the recently [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|decimated]] planet, Jebble. With Plaarvin gone, Jarael and Demagol were rescued and the ''Prospect'' left [[Metellos 3]]<ref name="KOTOR37:PM2" /> to investigate a missing ship, the ''[[Chancellor Fillorean]]''.<ref name="KOTOR38:FE">[[Knights of the Old Republic 38|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 38]]</ref>

Slyssk hotwired the hatches of the ''Chancellor Fillorean'' to gain entry, only for the crew to find it with all hands [[Death/Legends|dead]] bar two: [[Toki Tollivar]], a masquerading [[Bimm (furred species)/Legends|Bimm]] [[Sith acolyte/Legends|Sith Acolyte]]; and his [[K-OB7|companion droid]]. Slyssk gave the Bimm [[Water/Legends|water]] and prepared food, but when the Trandoshan was left alone with Tollivar, the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] attempted to [[Telekinesis/Legends#Choking powers|strangle]] him. Demagol performed a field tracheotomy, saving the Trandoshan's life, before Tollivar was exposed as a Sith and killed.<ref name="KOTOR38:FE" /> The ''Hot Prospect'' then traveled to the [[Swoopdueling/Legends|swoopdueling]] tournaments at [[Jervo's World]] where Slyssk was forced to remain medicated on the ship<ref name="KOTOR39:DA1">[[Knights of the Old Republic 39|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 39]]</ref> under Demagol's care while he recovered from Tollivar's attack.<ref name="KOTOR47:Demon1" /><ref name="47notes" />

===Chasing the Crucible===
{{Quote|With Slyssk working his wonders in the kitchen! Everyone who's anyone in the Republic wants to be seen here!|Marn Hierogrpyh, hosting the Goodvalor's Little Bivoli restaurant|<ref name="KOTOR50:Demon4">[[Knights of the Old Republic 50|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 50]]</ref>}}
The ''Hot Prospect'' next stopped off at [[Wor Tandell/Legends|Wor Tandell]], where Slyssk knew a friend at a plantation who would let them use the plantation's facilities. Jedi Knight Alek, now going by the name Malak, met Jarael at the camp and Slyssk was on hand to cook a meal for the party. After a confrontation between Malak and Demagol, the crew left the planet to root out [[The Crucible]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaving]] operation.<ref name="KOTOR42:M">[[Knights of the Old Republic 42|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 42]]</ref> Meanwhile, Slyssk, continuing his role of ship cleaner by mopping up after Demagol's chemical experiments, found a [[Identichip/Legends|microtag]] that had been affixed to [[Exar Kun's lightsaber/Legends|Exar Kun's lightsaber]] which Demagol had extracted from [[nullification resin]].<ref name="KOTOR48:Demon2">[[Knights of the Old Republic 48|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 48]]</ref>

Arriving in the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]], Carrick and Jarael pretended to be Crucible traders and confronted the head of the [[Sungrazer cooperative]], [[Sariyah Budan]], and threatened to undercut Budan's [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operation by collecting the valuable [[Thorilide/Legends|thorilide]] crystals themselves. To lend credence to their threat, the rest of the ''Prospect''{{'s}} crew started harvesting crystals with Slyssk even working out how to use several of the mining systems on the Calipsan 560, including the [[Particle accelerator/Legends|particle accelerator]]. The Trandoshan also pointed out to Hierogryph that they could use the ship's on-board centrifuge to process the crystals on the ship for greater convenience. However, as soon as Carrick and Jarael started to free the enslaved [[Dustdiver|workers]], Crucible [[Fleet Captain/Legends|Fleet Captain]] [[Dace Golliard]] arrived aboard the [[Gladiator (Crucible)|''Gladiator'']] to bring a halt to the disruption.<ref name="KOTOR43:TR1">[[Knights of the Old Republic 43|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 43]]</ref> With the Crucible ship laying down heavy [[Blaster/Legends|blasterfire]] on the ''Prospect''{{'s}} attitude thrusters, Slyssk was unable to move the ship around to retaliate with what little weaponry it possessed. Improvising, the crew used [[Xenoboric acid/Legends|xenoboric acid]], a drill and a crane arm to attack the ''Gladiator''. To make their escape, Carrick told Slyssk to shut off the [[Acceleration compensator/Legends|inertial compensators]] while he jammed the massive centrifuge aboard the ''Prospect''. The resulting transfer of momentum sent the mining ship into a sudden spin, allowing it to get clear enough to jump to hyperspace.<ref name="KOTOR44:TR2">[[Knights of the Old Republic 44|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 44]]</ref>

[[File:SlysskGoodvalorWaiter-KOTOR50.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Slyssk waiting tables on Coruscant]]

In an attempt to track down the Crucible's [[Dueling Pits|base]], Carrick posed as "[[Carth Onasi|Carth]] [[Kamlin]]," a stranded Republic Navy officer, and was taken to the planet [[Volgax]]. Jarael eventually managed to discover Carrick's location after the Crucible abandoned Volgax, but a disagreement over Jarael's past left the crew of the ''Hot Prospect'' bereft of both her and Demagol.<ref name="KOTOR46:Destroyer2">[[Knights of the Old Republic 46|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 46]]</ref> Meanwhile, on Coruscant, Rohlan Dyre—widely believed to actually be Demagol—awoke from the coma he had been in from when Demagol slipped aboard Carrick's ship prior to the Jedi and his companions meeting Slyssk. Carrick, Hierogryph and Slyssk were asked to return to Coruscant so that they could give evidence at [[Trial of Demagol|Demagol's trial]] for the many crimes and grotesque experiments he had carried out. However, before the trial, Carrick managed to get Hierogryph and Slyssk to reveal what had actually happened to them on Serroco. In a similar revelation, [[T1-LB]]—a [[LB-series bulk-loading droid|bulk-loading droid]] that had been traveling with Carrick—explained that Demagol had switched places with Dyre so that the latter was facing trial for the former's crimes. After breaking Dyre free from his trial as Demagol,<ref name="KOTOR47:Demon1" /> Carrick sent Slyssk to collect a set of [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]] for Dyre as well as to escort [[Shel Jelavan]], a co-founder of Carrick's [[Rogue Moon Project]], back to the [[Rogue Moon Project Command|base]] of the Project where the group formed a plan to find and rescue Jarael.<ref name="KOTOR48:Demon2" /> Slyssk piloted the ''Hot Prospect'' for a final [[Incident near Ithor system|mission]] in which Dace Golliard was captured. Golliard subsequently surrendered the location of where Jarael and Demagol were: the planet [[Osadia]].<ref name="KOTOR49:Demon3">[[Knights of the Old Republic 49|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 49]]</ref> On Osadia, Carrick and Dyre managed to save Jarael while Demagol was killed, bringing an end to the dangerous work he carried out. [[Standard day/Legends|Days]] later, the group went their own separate ways, with Slyssk working under Hierogryph as a waiter and chef in the [[Goodvalor's Little Bivoli]] restaurant on Coruscant.<ref name="KOTOR50:Demon4" />
